name: St. Claude Avenue Bridge — Camp Deck
description: The concrete deck of the double-leaf bascule span sits twenty-two feet above the flood plane, its surface radiating stored heat at 4:45 PM as eighty survivors arrange themselves in the load-bearing configurations established on the first morning. The smell is salt marsh, diesel, and sun-baked organic matter; there is no shade and no wind.
atmosphericDetails: The concrete deck has been holding heat since 7:00 AM and it is not releasing it; the surface temperature through boot soles is measurable
Eighty people arranged in clusters — the specific spacing of a population that has learned to distribute its weight without being told why
From the northern railing, the Industrial Canal stretches south toward the levee breach site; the water is the color of setting concrete and absolutely still
A skiff with a red-cross crate knocks against the piling six feet below the deck edge, its motor idling
The southern maintenance panel, if you know to look for it, sits flush with the deck surface near the counterweight housing
